text.title: Consistency and Standards Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Consistency and Standards. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Audit checks both internal consistency (within the product) and external consistency (with platform conventions); ignoring either creates a relearning tax.
rule.rationale: Internal-only consistency creates platform-divergent products; external-only creates internally chaotic ones.

rule.statement: Every recurring action (save, delete, share) uses one component, one label, one shortcut across the product.
rule.rationale: Pattern duplication is the most common heuristic-4 violation.

rule.statement: iOS and Android conventions are honoured by default; intentional divergence requires a written rationale.
rule.rationale: Silent divergence confuses users on their primary platform.

rule.statement: Approved terminology is enforced by linting; ad-hoc synonyms in microcopy are flagged for review.
rule.rationale: Synonym drift makes navigation and search unreliable.

rule.statement: Audit references components in the design system, not pixel-level details; component-grain catches drift earlier.
rule.rationale: Pixel audits are noisy; component audits catch real divergence.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-consistency-standards.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
